title: Top 5 Infectious Disease News Stories Week of March 29-April 5
summary: Development of a monoclonal antibody targeting mucormycosis, WHO’s call for improved fungal diagnostics and therapies, Bayesian dosing to optimize antimicrobial therapy, and more.

title: A Week of Swimming in Cold Water Can Change You on a Cellular Level
summary: If you've ever been cold water swimming, you'll know all about the short, sharp shock it gives your senses as you plunge in.

title: Could an inexpensive vaccine help stave off dementia?
summary: Millions of people suffer from dementia, and treatment options are scarce. A new study points in a fresh direction.

title: 3 common vitamin supplements that may be harming your health, expert reveals unexpected risks
summary: Not all vitamin supplements are safe, as some can harm your gut and overall health. Synthetic multivitamins and Vitamin B12 can irritate the gut, while additives like magnesium stearate interfere with nutrient absorption. Opt for supplements from natural sources and whole foods for better health benefits.

title: Test Shows Alcohol Causing Long-Term Brain Damage
summary: Scientists have found, for the first time, proof of just how bad alcohol impairs cognition — even when sober.

title: 5 Mediterranean Diet Foods to Stock Up On to Lose Weight
summary: Dietitians say the best Mediterranean diet weight loss foods are lentils, Greek yogurt, chickpeas, nuts and spinach. Learn why plus tasty recipes.

title: Increased screen time can increase risk of depression in teen girls, finds study
summary: The new study found that increased screen time led to deteriorated sleep within three months, impacting both the duration and quality of sleep. The study was conducted by researchers from Sweden's Karolinska Institutet and published in the journal PLOS Global Public Health. Read on to know more.

title: Increased screen time found to increase depression risk in teen girls by disrupting sleep, study finds
summary: Mental Health Risks: Excessive screen time negatively affects sleep quality and duration, increasing the risk of depressive symptoms, especially in teenage girls. A study involving Swedish students highlights the significant role of screen-induced sleep disturbances in contributing to depression, with varying impacts on boys and girls.

title: Researchers Develop Antiviral Chewing Gum That Reduces Herpes And Flu Viral Infections By Over 95%
summary: A breakthrough antiviral chewing gum developed by University of Pennsylvania researchers shows promise in significantly reducing herpes and influenza virus transmission.

title: Welcome to the Worst Allergy Season Ever
summary: Multiple US states have logged record pollen counts this spring, with climate change likely to blame.
